2606.25434.title: Interpretable Concept-Guided Polynomial Tabular Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for EEG-Based Mild Cognitive Impairment Detection
2606.25434.paper_summary: Early and scalable detection of mild cognitive impairment (MCI) remains an unresolved clinical challenge. Existing EEG-based screening approaches are constrained by handcrafted feature pipelines that discard neurophysiologically meaningful domain structure and deep learning classifiers that sacrifice interpretability for performance. No existing work unifies physiologically organized concept encoders, cross-concept interaction modeling, and nonlinear tabular classification in a sleep EEG-based MCI detection framework. This study proposes Concept-guided Polynomial-transformed Tabular learning using Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (CPTabKAN), which maps heterogeneous EEG-derived features into domain-informed concept representations, expands them via degree-2 polynomial transformation to expose first- and second-order interactions, and applies a Fourier-parameterized TabKAN classifier to learn nonlinear decision boundaries. CPTabKAN was evaluated on the Study of Osteoporotic Fractures cohort (372 subjects, overnight polysomnography), using 1,379 features organized into ten physiologically motivated concept groups. Under 10-fold cross-validation, CPTabKAN-Second Order achieved a weighted F1-score of 0.9038 (SD 0.034), outperforming GradientBoosting by 5.65 percentage points (t(9)=1.934,p=0.043, one-sided paired test), with advantages persisting under SMOTE-based balancing. Ablation analysis confirmed independent contributions from each component. Concept importance analysis revealed that power spectral density, multi-scale entropy, and Hjorth parameters dominated first-order weights, while cross-concept interactions involving Lempel-Ziv-Welch complexity, statistics, demographics, and slow oscillations exceeded all first-order scores. These results demonstrate that concept-structured, interaction-aware tabular learning surfaces physiologically coherent reasoning, supporting clinical trust.
2606.25434.paper_summary_zh: 早期且可擴展的輕度認知障礙（MCI）檢測仍然是一個未解決的臨床挑戰。現有的基於腦電圖（EEG）的篩查方法受到手工特徵管道的限制，這些管道忽略了神經生理學上有意義的領域結構，以及深度學習分類器在性能上犧牲可解釋性。現有的研究沒有將生理組織的概念編碼器、跨概念互動建模和非線性表格分類統一到基於睡眠EEG的MCI檢測框架中。本研究提出了使用Kolmogorov-Arnold網絡的概念引導多項式變換表格學習（CPTabKAN），該方法將異質的EEG衍生特徵映射到領域知情的概念表示中，通過二次多項式變換擴展它們以揭示一級和二級互動，並應用傅里葉參數化的TabKAN分類器來學習非線性決策邊界。CPTabKAN在骨質疏鬆性骨折研究隊列（372名受試者，過夜多導睡眠檢查）上進行了評估，使用了1,379個特徵，這些特徵組織成十個生理驅動的概念組。在10倍交叉驗證下，CPTabKAN-二級達到了0.9038的加權F1分數（標準差0.034），比GradientBoosting高出5.65個百分點（t(9)=1.934,p=0.043，單側配對檢驗），在基於SMOTE的平衡下仍然保持優勢。消融分析確認了每個組件的獨立貢獻。概念重要性分析顯示，功率譜密度、多尺度熵和Hjorth參數主導了一級權重，而涉及Lempel-Ziv-Welch複雜性、統計數據、人口統計學和慢振盪的跨概念互動超過了所有一級分數。這些結果表明，結構化的概念、互動感知的表格學習展現了生理上連貫的推理，支持臨床信任。
2606.25434.author: Yosef Bernardus Wirian et.al.
